enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song Peiyophobilia?

The song Peiyophobilia explores themes of fear and excitement, blending humor with a lively beat.

What is the context of the song in the movie Rum?

In Rum, this song captures the carefree spirit of the characters, enhancing the film's energetic vibe.

Who are the artists behind the song?

The song features vocals by Anirudh and Simbu, with music composed by Anirudh himself.

When was Peiyophobilia released?

Peiyophobilia was released on September 23, 2016.
enrichedMetadata.summary: The song Peiyophobilia from the movie Rum is an energetic duet that perfectly encapsulates the carefree and vibrant spirit of youth. With its catchy beats and playful lyrics, it conveys a sense of excitement and joy, making it a perfect party anthem. The collaboration between Anirudh and Simbu adds a unique flavor, enhancing the song's appeal. Overall, it evokes feelings of happiness and celebration, making it a memorable track in the film's soundtrack.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Peiyophobilia from Rum is a vibrant track that showcases the electrifying collaboration between Anirudh and Simbu. With its catchy beats and lively lyrics, this song is sure to get you on your feet. Dive into the world of energetic rhythms and unforgettable melodies that define this hit!
stanzaMeanings: A person who is scared of women is losing his life.
He won't even let a shadow of a woman pass by in this world.
He is a dead man, but he still holds onto that fear, and we are left to deal with it.
He posts as if he is fresh, but he will throw it all away in the end.
This is Peiyophobilia, a fear that makes us anxious; trust me, you are wrong, mango, wrong, wrong.
Fear is a terrible thing; once you understand it, it becomes a joke.
In the darkness, nothing is scary; it's just a knife, like a person named Kathiresan.
Don't be a fool, don't live like a loser.
Don't go without a trace; don't just disappear.
Your life is going well, but you are searching for ghosts that aren't there.
Even if you lose your mind, you will be shocked and run away.
This is Peiyophobilia, a fear that makes us anxious; trust me, you are wrong, mango, wrong, wrong.
A person who is scared of women is losing his life.

This is Peiyophobilia, a fear that makes us anxious; trust me, you are wrong, mango, wrong, wrong.
